user_query:
I need our landing page copy localized into Spanish (es-ES) and French (fr-FR) in a way we can audit. Please use only the files in this workspace—no external APIs. Deliverables and constraints:

Inputs to use:
- input/brand_voice.md (tone and what not to translate)
- input/products.md (English source copy with placeholders)
- input/glossary.csv (approved term translations)
- config/localization.yml and scripts/translate.py (provided stubs)

What to change:
1) Update config/localization.yml to add both of the following keys so they are used by the script:
   - preserve_terms: ["Northstar Capital", "Northstar Flex"]
   - placeholder_patterns: ["\\{[^}]+\\}"]
   Leave locales, output_dir, and source_files as is.

2) Modify scripts/translate.py so that, when run, it:
   - Reads input/products.md and input/glossary.csv, applies translations for es-ES and fr-FR using the glossary with case-preserving whole-word matching (e.g., if the English term is Title Case, the translated phrase should also be Title Case; UPPERCASE stays UPPERCASE; lowercase stays lowercase).
   - Preserves any tokens matching placeholder_patterns exactly (e.g., {business_name}, {website_url}, {first_name}).
   - Leaves preserve_terms from the config (e.g., "Northstar Capital", "Northstar Flex") unchanged in all locales.
   - Translates the rest of the English content in products.md into Spanish and French in keeping with input/brand_voice.md. It is acceptable to implement rules-based or manual translation for the supplied content as long as the above constraints are enforced.
   - Writes localized files to:
     - output/es-ES/products.md
     - output/fr-FR/products.md
   - Produces a QA report at output/qa/report.json with, for each locale, the following fields: source (string), output (string), lines_total (int), lines_changed (int), glossary_matches (int), preserved_placeholders (int), preserved_terms (int), md5 (string; MD5 of the output file contents).
   - Produces unified diff files comparing source to each localized file at:
     - output/qa/diff-es-ES.txt
     - output/qa/diff-fr-FR.txt
   - Prints the absolute paths of all generated artifacts to stdout when complete.

3) Run the script from the command line (e.g., `python scripts/translate.py`) so the artifacts above are created under the output/ directory.

Success criteria:
- The two localized markdown files exist at the required paths and follow the glossary, placeholder, and preserve_terms rules.
- The QA report JSON exists with non-null values and metrics that recompute from the generated files (e.g., MD5 hash matches the actual file, counts align with diffs).
- The diff files exist and reflect changed lines.

This needs to be reliable, auditable, and aligned with our brand voice so we can ship this in our next campaign. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
